text.title: growth-customer-testimonials Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-customer-testimonials as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using fake or heavily fabricated testimonials.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Catastrophically reputationally damaging if exposed. Only use real, approved quotes from real customers. Zero exceptions.

failure-mode.description: Placing all testimonials in a single section at the bottom of the page.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users who reach the bottom are already past the primary decision points. Testimonials below the fold assist only the most motivated readers, not the majority who abandon earlier.

failure-mode.description: Publishing stale testimonials (from customers who have churned, with old company logos, outdated roles).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale testimonials signal an inactive customer base. Prospects notice date signals. Refresh testimonials annually.

failure-mode.description: Using only one format of social proof (only quotes, no logos, no numbers, no case studies).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Different proof types resonate with different buyers at different stages. A single format leaves trust-building opportunities on the table.
